headline: What role did France play in the American Revolution?
description: On July 4, Americans are celebrating the 250th anniversary of their independence – an event in which the French played a part.

headline: France beats Paraguay 1-0, advances to World Cup quarter-finals
Michael Olise, France's heaven-sent prodigy
Climate change: 'We cannot meet the challenge of adapting housing with the tools of the past. A thorough overhaul is needed'
description: Christophe Rodriguez, the director of the French Institute for Building Energy Performance, explains in an interview with Le Monde the urgent need to adapt institutions in order to carry out the 'project of the century' – the renovation of France's building stock.

headline: 'We sacrificed everything': In Nablus, the relentless decline of Palestinian society
description: In the occupied West Bank, Israel's financial stranglehold on the Palestinian Authority, combined with the proliferation of military checkpoints, has pushed society into unprecedented poverty. In Nablus, the economic heart of the occupied territory, all sectors have faced a sharp decline in living standards.

headline: Paul Seixas, the 19-year-old phenom carrying France's Tour hopes
description: For his first appearance at the Tour de France, which begins Saturday in Barcelona, the 19-year-old French cyclist is already seen as one of the top rivals to four-time winner Tadej Pogacar.

headline: China's bridge and highway construction boom runs up against wall of debt in Guizhou
description: A symbol of China's ambitious infrastructure policy, the southern province of Guizhou has seen mega-bridges, airports and new neighborhoods proliferate. This excessive surge has also resulted in unmanageable government debt, corruption scandals and unfinished construction projects.

'Which side were you on during Brexit? Every candidate in the French presidential election should answer this question'
description: The lessons from the UK's unprecedented vote to leave the European Union 10 years ago have been all too quickly forgotten, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

headline: Four graphs to visualize the unprecedented scale of France's June heatwave
description: France's most recent heatwave began on June 17 and reached unprecedented levels in terms of both peak temperatures and official alert status. It proved to be more intense than the infamous 2003 heatwave and could also surpass its duration.

headline: The Christian right's charge to rewrite American history
description: 'America 250' (12/13). Barack Obama's election in 2008 led to greater recognition of the role of minorities in nation-building. But the conservative backlash soon followed. Leading the 'patriotic' counter-offensive, Donald Trump has received support from the influential Hillsdale College.
